heading: Interim control of hazardous waste injection
label: 6939b Interim control of hazardous waste injection

children.heading: Underground source of drinking water
children.label: a Underground source of drinking water

children.text: into a formation which contains (within one-quarter mile of the well used for such underground injection) an underground source of drinking water; or

children.heading: Actions under Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act
children.label: b Actions under Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act
A
children.text: a response action taken under section 9604 or 9606 of this title, or

children.label: B
children.text: part of corrective action required under this chapter1So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.1
such contaminated ground water is treated to substantially reduce hazardous constituents prior to such injection; and
such response action or corrective action will, upon completion, be sufficient to protect human health and the environment.

which has adopted identical or more stringent prohibitions under part C of the Safe Drinking Water Act [ et seq.] and which has assumed primary enforcement responsibility under that Act for enforcement of such prohibitions; or42 U.S.C. 300h

in which the Administrator has adopted identical or more stringent prohibitions under the Safe Drinking Water Act [ et seq.] and is exercising primary enforcement responsibility under that Act for enforcement of such prohibitions.42 U.S.C. 300f

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: d Definitions
children.text: The terms “primary enforcement responsibility”, “underground source of drinking water”, “formation” and “well” have the same meanings as provided in regulations of the Administrator under the Safe Drinking Water Act [ et seq.]. The term “Safe Drinking Water Act” means title XIV of the Public Health Service Act.42 U.S.C. 300f
